headline: Samsung and SK Hynix tumble 7%+ as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
detail: This is the most direct threat to your book. Korean chip giants cratering signals the global semiconductor sell-off has legs beyond just US names. Your SOXL, SMH, and indirectly QQQ and SPY all feel this - SOXL gets hit 3X on any further chip weakness.
rec: Seriously consider trimming SOXL if it approaches $200 stop - don't wait for it to slice through on margin. SMH stop at $540 is your broader chip line in the sand; respect it.

headline: Russia launches massive strike on Ukraine, Poland scrambles jets, Finland restricts airspace
detail: Major escalation in European conflict drives risk-off flows globally. High-beta, levered tech names get sold first in genuine geopolitical flare-ups. Adds headwind on top of already-weak chip sentiment today.
rec: No specific trade from this alone, but it reinforces holding cash/staying defensive. Don't add new longs into a dual risk-off catalyst day - let price settle first.

headline: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure
detail: If this deal gets traction it's broadly positive for AI infrastructure sentiment - good for names like CRWD and the QQQ complex longer term. But it's a report, not confirmed, so don't chase it today.
rec: No action today - CRWD is already your strongest hold at +7.4% with stop well below at $590. If AI sentiment improves on confirmed news, that's the position already working for you.

headline: BYD and Xiaomi surge on June delivery figures
detail: Chinese EV/consumer tech outperformance is sector-specific and doesn't directly move your holdings, but strong Chinese tech can lift global risk appetite at the margin and support SMH/QQQ if it sustains.
rec: No action - none of your holdings have direct BYD or Xiaomi exposure, and the chip rout headline today outweighs any positive spillover from Chinese delivery beats.
